pattern: Binary search still narrows the window, but ties between the midpoint and the right end force a one-step shrink, since duplicates hide which half holds the minimum.
hints: Duplicates can make the middle equal to the right end, leaving you unable to tell which side dropped.
When nums[mid] is clearly greater than nums[hi] the minimum is to the right; when clearly smaller it is at mid or left; equality is the ambiguous case.
On nums[mid] > nums[hi] set lo = mid + 1; on nums[mid] < nums[hi] set hi = mid; on equality just do hi -= 1 to safely discard one duplicate.

edge_inputs.description: duplicates on both ends, minimum buried

stress.description: large array of values from a small range sorted then rotated, many duplicates
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 5000))
    vals = [rng.randrange(-20, 21) for _ in range(n)]
    vals.sort()
    k = rng.randrange(1, n + 1)
    rotated = vals[k % n:] + vals[:k % n]
    return [rotated]
stress.note: small value range guarantees heavy duplication, stressing the equality branch
